title: Quantum Precoded Polar Codes
authors: Tyler Kann, Shrinivas Kudekar, Matthieu R. Bloch
abstract: We introduce a new family of CSS codes obtained from rate-1 precoded polar codes, which harnesses the precoding benefits obtained for classical short blocklength polar codes. We optimize the rate profile and precoder of these codes with a genetic algorithm, and present codes of dimension $ [\![256, 2 ]\!] $ and $ [\![512, 2]\!] $ that have logical error rates similar to the $ [\![1201, 1, 25 ]\!] $ surface code over the depolarizing channel.
summary: This paper introduces a new class of quantum error-correcting codes that use precoding techniques from classical information theory to enhance polar codes at short blocklengths. These codes can match the error suppression of much larger surface codes while using significantly fewer qubits, making them highly efficient for near-term quantum hardware.
main_problem: Improving the performance of quantum polar codes in the short blocklength regime by adapting classical rate-1 precoding techniques while satisfying the CSS (Calderbank-Shor-Steane) construction constraints.
main_result: The authors developed a new family of quantum precoded CSS codes that achieve logical error rates comparable to much larger surface codes, offering a significant boost in code rate and qubit efficiency.
method: A genetic algorithm is used to optimize the information sets and the precoding matrix under CSS constraints, combined with a joint quaternary Successive Cancellation List (SCL) decoder.
model_or_system: The study focuses on quantum error-correcting codes (specifically CSS and polar codes) operating under a depolarizing noise channel.
key_observables: Logical error rate, physical error rate, code rate, and gate complexity (Clifford gate count).
important_parameters: Blocklength (N=64, 128, 256, 512), code dimension (K), and the physical error rate (p).
main_assumptions: The precoder must be an involution and persymmetric to maintain the CSS property; the noise is modeled by a standard depolarizing channel.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares the logical error rates of the proposed precoded codes against the surface code; Figure 2 illustrates the performance improvement of a code through successive stages of the genetic algorithm optimization.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the quantum precoding concept, details the CSS-constrained optimization via a genetic algorithm, describes the joint quaternary decoding method, and concludes with a performance comparison against the surface code in terms of error rates and circuit complexity.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1. A comparison between JN, 2K precoded polar codes for N = 256, 512 and J1201, 1, 25K surface code with χ = 4, 6. The J256, 2, L = 8, 32K codes are two different codes from Algorithm 1, as opposed to one code evaluated at two list sizes.
Fig. 2. The J64, 2, L = 4K code seen through the stages of Algorithm 1, and compared against the J128, 2, L = 4K polar code. A0 is the profile from [7].
